Q9 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. If only shear stress is acting then volume of the specimen does not change.
2. The ultimate shear strength observed from shear stress-strain curve is almost the same as ultimate normal strength observed from normal stress-strain curve.
3. For an isotropic and homogeneous material, number of independent and distinct elastic constants are 21.
Which of the statement are CORRECT?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
For isotropic and homogeneous material, number of independent elastic constants is 2 i.e. (E and μ).
If body are stressed by shear stress as in pure shear case, then the change in volume of stressed
body is zero.

Q21 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In slope deflection equation, deformations are considered to be caused by

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
In slope-deflection equations, deformations (specifically angular and transverse displacements)
are primarily considered to be caused by bending moment because the method is a displacement
method that directly relates moments to rotations and deflections, while neglecting the effects of
axial and shear forces on these angular deformations.

Q23 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Factor load method of approximate analysis deals with vertical loads only.
2. Portal method and cantilever method of approximate analysis deal with horizontal loads.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are INCORRECT?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The approximate “factor method” is used for analyzing multistory frames subjected to horizontal
or lateral loads, such as wind and seismic forces. The analysis for vertical loads is typically done
using other approximate methods, like the substitute frame method.
The portal method and cantilever method are both approximate analysis techniques specifically
for frames subjected to horizontal loads.

Q26 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct about conditions in plastic analysis?
1. When a mechanism forms, the ultimate load is reached.
2. In equilibrium condition, mechanism condition and yield condition are satisfied and a unique value of highest plastic limit, is obtained.
3. In yield condition, bending moment at any section must not exceed the full plastic moment of the section.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
In equilibrium condition, mechanism condition and yield condition are satisfied and a unique
value i.e. the lowest plastic limit, is obtained. This is also called kinematic method of plastic
analysis.

Q29 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Intermediate vertical stiffeners are provided in the plate girders to

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Intermediate vertical stiffeners in plate girders are primarily designed to prevent the web from
buckling under shear stresses. Buckling occurs when a thin plate experiences excessive compression,
causing it to deform and lose stability. These stiffeners divide the web into smaller panels,
increasing its resistance to buckling and maintaining the structural integrity of the girder.

Q30 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Splices are provided when the available length is higher than the required length of a tension member.
2. In design of a tension splice, the effect of eccentricity is neglected as far as possible.
3. If sections to spliced are not of same thickness, packings are introduced.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Splices are used to join two sections of a member when the available length of a single rolled
section is less than the required length.

Q31 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Local buckling in beam can controlled by:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Local buckling in beams is controlled by the width to thickness ratio of each element of the crosssection.
A lower width-to-thickness ratio makes an element more resistant to local buckling, as a
smaller ratio signifies a thicker, more stable plate element relative to its width.

Not attempted

The splicing of reinforcement bars in RCC beam can be done at a section where

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

exceeds 50% of the moment of resistance at that section. This means the bending moment must be
less than one half of the maximum bending moment.

Q48 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Free float affects only the succeeding activity.
2. Independent float affects only the particular concerned activity.
3. Total float affects both successive and preceding activity.
Which of the above statements are CORRECT?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Independent float is the amount of time by which an activity can be delayed without affecting
any preceding or succeeding activities. It is the most restricted type of float and is defined by
a delay that does not impact any other activity in the project network.
• Total float is the amount of time by which an activity can be delayed without delaying the
project's overall completion date. Though total float can affect the floats of both preceding
and succeeding activities in the same path, as it consumes a shared buffer time.

Q49 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are CORRECT regarding ladder network?
1. It is used to isolate repetitive works.
2. It enables uninterrupted utilization of equipment.
3. It is difficult to find activity on critical path.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• Ladder networks are used in project management to model and manage projects that involve
repetitive activities, such as those in construction.
• This technique, also referred to as "laddering" is a form of multi-tasking where work can
continue without interruption, allowing for the continuous use of resources.
• The use of a ladder network can make identifying the critical path difficult. It can even lead
to more than one critical path, which increases the complexity and risk of the project schedule.

Q51 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
The salient features of a bar chart over network are that
1. It is simple to draw and easy to understand.
2. It is unable to depict interdependencies of activities.
3. It clearly distinguishes between critical and non-critical activities.
4. It is not possible to crash activities to get optimum and minimum duration of the project.
Which of the above statements are CORRECT?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The salient features of bar chart are:
(i) Simple to draw and easy to understand
(ii) Unable to depict interdependencies
(iii) Cannot distinguish critical and non-critical activities
(iv) Not possible to crash activities

Q52 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. One of the disadvantage of bar chart is that it does not depict the interdependencies of the activities.
2. In work breakdown structure, top-down approach is adopted.
Which of the above statement(s) is/are INCORRECT.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Limitations of bar chart:
• Lack of degree of details.
• Review of project progress.
• It does not show interdependencies and relationship between various activities of the project.
• Time uncertainties.
• It does not indicate the critical activities of the project.
Work Breakdown Structure (WBS):
• Work Breakdown Structure is a graphical representation of functional elements of entire
project.
• It follows top to bottom approach.

Q53 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

A project consists of six activities (jobs) designated from A to F as shown below in the network diagram

 
In reference to above network diagram, which of the following relationships are correct?
1. B and C can be performed concurrently and must follow A.
2. B must precede D.
3. E must precede C, but it cannot be started until B is complete.
4. F is the last operation and depends on the completion of only activity D.
Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• E must succeed C, but it cannot be started until B is complete.
• F is the last operation and depends on the completion of both D and E.

Q58 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Negative value of cost variance (CV) of construction project indicates that project is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Cost Variance (CV) is a metric used in project management to measure financial performance.
It is calculated by subtracting the actual cost (AC) from the earned value (EV) of the work
performed (CV = EV – AC).
• A negative value of cost variance indicates that the actual cost is greater than the earned
value, meaning the project has spend more money than was budgeted for the work. Therefore,
the project is exceeding the budget.

Q70 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

For a constant water-cement ratio, a decrease in aggregate-cement ratio causes

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
For a constant water-cement ratio, an increase in aggregate-cement ratio will decrease the
workability. Also more cementitious material is needed when finer aggregate grading is used.
On the other hand, on decreasing aggregate-cement ratio will increase the workability of concrete
as more cement is available to lubricate the aggregates.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

a depth 'Z' under a circular footing, carrying a uniform load of intensity 'q'. This chart incidentally
can be used not only for circular footing but for all other types of footing like square, rectangular
or any other shape making it a very useful tool in our hands.

Q99 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Air-binding in rapid sand filters is encountered when

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The negative pressure in filter media tends to release the dissolved air and other gases present in water. The gas bubbles gets attached to the sand grains and seriously affects the working of the filter. This is called air binding. Algae may also produce oxygen and cause air binding. So algae growth should be controlled by using algicides.
Increase in water temperature reduces solubility of dissolved air and it gets released to cause air
binding. So this should be avoided.
The filter should be cleaned as soon as loss of head exceeds the optimum allowable values. Here
compressed air and water can be used.

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66

2. The primary disadvantage for the facultative pond is the high suspended-solids concentrations in the effluent.
Which of the above statements are TRUE?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

Q101 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The correct sequence with time of biomass growth for secondary treatment of wastewater is

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
In secondary wastewater treatment, the biomass growth follows a typical bacterial growth curve
with distinct phases:
• Lag phase: Initially, the bacteria adapt to the new environment and prepare for rapid growth,
with minimal cell division.
• Log phase (exponential phase): The bacteria actively divide and rapidly increase in number
due to optimal conditions.
• Stationary phase: The population growth slows down as the nutrient availability becomes
limited, and the rate of cell birth equals the rate of cell death.
• Endogenous phase: In some cases, after the stationary phase, there might be a decline in the
population due to nutrient depletion or accumulation of waste products.

Q102 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Best quality of water is to be drawn for public water supply scheme from a lake. What must is the required state of the lake?
1. Oligotrophic
2. Mesotrophic
3. Eutrophic
4. Senescent
Select the CORRECT answer using the codes given below

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Oligotrophic takes: Oligotrophic lakes have a low level of productivity due to severely limited
supply of nutrients to support algal growth
Mesotropic lakes: The lakes having medium productivity levels, with medium growth of algae
and turbidity are usually classified as mesotropic lakes,
Eutropic lakes: Eutropic lakes do have a high level of productivity because of abundant supply of
algal nutrients.
Senescent lakes: These are very old shallow lakes, having thick organic sediment deposits at their bottoms.

Not attempted

Electrostatic precipitator is a device to control

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Electrostatic precipitator used for large industrial plants particularly in thermal power plants are
voltage single stage units. There are four steps in their working: (i) Electrical charging of the
particulates. (ii) Collection of charged particles on a ground surface. (iii) Neutralization of the
charged at the collector. (iv) Removal of the particulate for disposal.

Q109 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following can be used as a map substitute?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Photomaps are the aerial photographs which are used as a substitute for maps. The photomap may consist of one photograph, but usually photomaps are obtained by assembling two or more photographs to form a large map.
The large photomaps from two or more photographs are called mosaics.
The mosaic has an overall average scale comparable to the scale of a planimetric map.

Q115 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements about theodolites:
1. Transit theodolite is a theodolite in which the telescope can be transited.
2. EDMI is a theodolite fitted with a micrometer for measurements.
3. A double reading theodolite is the one in which diametrically opposite segments of the graduated circle are brought into view and the readings are averaged.
Which of these statements are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
A theodolite is a precision instrument used for measuring angles both horizontally and vertically.
Theodolites can rotate along their horizontal axis as well as their vertical axis. A transit is a
surveying instrument that also takes accurate angular measurements. Transits use vernier scales
and external graduated metal circles for angular readings.
A double reading theodolite is the one in which images of diametrically opposite parts of the
graduated circle are brought into the field of view by a suitable optical arrangement and are read
and averaged in one stroke.

Q116 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following best defines a mineral and a rock?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
A mineral is defined by its specific internal crystal structure, where the atoms are arranged in a
regular, repeating pattern. A rock, on the other hand, is composed of various minerals that are
compacted and cemented together.

Q122 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

For horizontal alignment of pavement, spiral transition curve is preferred because
1. Rate of change of centrifugal acceleration is consistent (smooth).
2. Calculation and field implementation are easy
3. Radius of transition curve changes smoothly from infinite to radius of curve.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
1. Constant rate of change: The centrifugal acceleration experienced by a vehicle changes
uniformly along the spiral, which ensures a smooth and comfortable transition for passengers.
2. Gradual change in radius: The spiral curve connects a straight section (of infinite radius) to
a circular curve (of finite radius) by smoothly decreasing its own radius. This allows drivers
to gradually adjust their steering.
3. Simplicity in implementation: While its properties ensure ideal performance, the spiral
curve is also geometrically straightforward, which simplifies calculations and field
implementation.

Q123 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Select the correct statement regarding traffic rotary:

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Rotary is suitable when proportion of the right turn traffic is very high, typically when more than
30% of the total traffic is right-turning traffic. This is because rotaries convert severe conflicts to
milder merging/diverging movements, allowing vehicles to flow more smoothly when there is a
high volume of right turns.

Q124 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

For the design of runways, if the standard atmospheric temperature at sea level is 15°C, then the standard temperature at an altitude of 1 km will be

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Standard temperature = 0.0065 × 1000 = 6.5°C
∴ Temperature at an altitude of 1 km = (15 – 6.5 × 1)°C = 8.5°C.

Q129 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The least accurate method of computing mean depth of rainfall is

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The arithmetic mean method simply calculates the average rainfall by adding up all the rainfall
measurements from different stations and dividing by the total number of stations. This method
doesn't account for spatial variations in rainfall within the area, which can lead to inaccurate
estimations, especially when rainfall varies significantly across the region.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

methods, it's important to remember that these losses can't be entirely eliminated. Factors like
environmental conditions (humidity, temperature, wind speed) and system design can influence
the amount of water lost through evaporation and percolation from the soil surface and root zone.

Q140 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next Ten (10) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Plasticizer permits the reduction in water-cement ratio at the same workability.
Statement (II): Plasticizer acts as a deflocculating agent.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Plasticizers get adsorbed over the surface of cement particles and thereby create repulsion between
the particles and makes certain amount of entrapped water free for hydration process and thus,
acts as deflocculating agent.

Q141 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next Ten (10) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Crop period is slightly more than the base period for any crop.
Statement (II): Crop period is the period between the first watering and the last watering of the crop.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The time period that elapses from the instant of its sowing to the instant of its harvesting is called
the crop period while the time between first watering of a crop at the time of its sowing to its last
watering before harvesting is called the base period.

(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
HGL is not properly defined at the entrance due to loss at the upstream of reservoir.

(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Primary air pollutants often combine with other or with water vapours in the presence of sunlight and leads to formation of secondary air pollutants.
Statement (II): Photochemical smog is a mixture of ozone, formaldehydes and PAN.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Statement (I): Total shrinkage of concrete depends upon constituents of concrete, size of member and environmental conditions.
Statement (II): For a given humidity and temperature, the total shrinkage of concrete is most influenced by cement content.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
For a given humidity and temperature, the total shrinkage of concrete is most influenced by the
total amount of water present in the concrete at that time of mixing.

Correct answer: A
Solution

Statement (II): The tunnel length upto which natural draft can be expected to be sufficient for natural ventilation gets drastically reduced.

Solution

supposed to provide natural ventilation gets considerably reduced.

Q150 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next Ten (10) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): If the wall rotates about its toe, thus moving away from the backfill, the soil mass expands, resulting in a decrease of the earth pressure.
Statement (II): When wall moves away from backfill, the shear resistance mobilises in a direction opposing the movement of the earth mass.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
When the wall moves away from the backfill, a portion of the backfill located next to the retaining
wall tends to break away from the rest of the soil mass and tends to move downwards and
outwards relative to the wall. Since the shear resistance is mobilised in direction away from the
wall, there is a resultant decrease in the earth pressure.
